P004_S0024.reason: Related passages (arXiv version and figure caption context) show the term is '+ A_0\frac{1}{2}(1-3\,\cos^2 \theta^*)' with a plus sign, directly contradicting the perturbed minus sign.
P004_S0024.original:
\begin{multline}
\frac{\rd^2\sigma}{\rd\theta^*\rd\phi^*} \propto 
(1+\cos^2 \theta^*) 
+ A_0\frac{1}{2}(1-3\,\cos^2 \theta^*) \\
+ A_1\sin(2\theta^*)\cos\phi^* 
+ A_2\frac{1}{2}\sin^2 \theta^*\cos(2\phi^*) \\
+ A_3\sin \theta^*\cos \phi^* 
+ A_4\cos \theta^* 
+ A_5\sin^2 \theta^*\sin(2\phi^*) \\
+ A_6\sin(2\theta^*)\sin\phi^* 
+ A_7\sin\phi^*\sin\theta^*.
\label{DoubleDiffCrossSection}
\end{multline}
P004_S0024.perturbed:
\begin{multline}
\frac{\rd^2\sigma}{\rd\theta^*\rd\phi^*} \propto 
(1+\cos^2 \theta^*) 
- A_0\frac{1}{2}(1-3\,\cos^2 \theta^*) \\
+ A_1\sin(2\theta^*)\cos\phi^* 
+ A_2\frac{1}{2}\sin^2 \theta^*\cos(2\phi^*) \\
+ A_3\sin \theta^*\cos \phi^* 
+ A_4\cos \theta^* 
+ A_5\sin^2 \theta^*\sin(2\phi^*) \\
+ A_6\sin(2\theta^*)\sin\phi^* 
+ A_7\sin\phi^*\sin\theta^*.
\label{DoubleDiffCrossSection}
\end{multline}
